verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane cargo receptor of the p24 family that cycles between the ER and Golgi, with documented minority pools reaching the plasma membrane through the secretory pathway. Surface display has been reported (e.g., on certain immune and tumor cells), and it functions in cargo sorting/COPI-COPII cycling. The dominant compartment is endomembrane (ER/Golgi), but a PM-accessible pool exists via vesicular cycling, qualifying for dual_localization. Not GPI/multipass; ectodomain is small but topologically extracellular when at PM.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether the PM minority pool is sufficiently stable/abundant to support binder engagement vs being a transient transit intermediate.
